data.name: Atbash Cipher
data.description: Encrypt and decrypt text with the Atbash cipher by mirroring letters across the selected alphabet. One of the oldest known substitution ciphers in classical cryptography.
data.meta_description: Use the Atbash cipher online. Mirror letters across the alphabet, encrypt or decrypt text instantly, and explore one of the oldest classical ciphers.

data.title: How Atbash cipher works
data.text:
The Atbash cipher replaces every letter with its opposite position in the alphabet. In the English alphabet, A becomes Z, B becomes Y, C becomes X, and so on.

Unlike most classical ciphers, Atbash does not require a key. The substitution is fixed and always follows the same mirrored alphabet pattern.

Atbash is self-reciprocal, meaning the same operation is used for both encryption and decryption. Applying the cipher twice restores the original text.

data.title: History of the Atbash cipher
data.text:
Atbash originated in the ancient Hebrew alphabet and is considered one of the earliest known substitution ciphers. It was used by reversing the order of alphabet letters and replacing each symbol with its mirrored counterpart.

Today, Atbash is primarily used for education, puzzles, and historical demonstrations of classical cryptography.

data.question: Is Atbash secure for modern use?
data.answer: No. Atbash is a classical monoalphabetic substitution and is easy to break with frequency analysis.

data.question: Why are encryption and decryption identical?
data.answer: Because Atbash uses a perfectly mirrored alphabet. Applying the same substitution a second time returns every letter to its original position.

data.question: Can I work with non-Latin alphabets?
data.answer: Yes. Choose the appropriate alphabet in settings and the tool will mirror letters within it.

data.question: Does Atbash use a secret key?
data.answer: No. Atbash uses a fixed mirrored alphabet and does not require a key or password.

data.question: What is the difference between Atbash and Caesar cipher?
data.answer: Caesar shifts letters by a chosen number of positions, while Atbash always mirrors letters across the alphabet. Atbash therefore requires no key or configuration.

data.question: Can I preserve spaces and punctuation?
data.answer: Yes. Spaces, punctuation marks, and numbers remain unchanged while letters are mirrored.

data.title: Encrypt HELLO WORLD
data.description: Alphabet: English. Atbash replaces letters with mirrored positions.

data.title: Decrypt SVOOL DLIOW
data.description: Alphabet: English. Applying Atbash again restores original text.

data.title: Preserve spaces and punctuation
data.description: Letters are mirrored while spaces and punctuation remain unchanged.

data.title: Mirror the entire alphabet
data.description: A complete Atbash alphabet mapping example.

notes: Сохраняйте id у каждой сущности без изменений.
Менять нужно только текстовые поля в data.

В blocks.data.text используйте HTML-разметку: минимум один

, можно несколько

; также допустимы списки

    /
      с
    1. .
Пустые строки допустимы: при импорте это удалит перевод для конкретного языка.
Для добавления новых элементов в sections blocks/faq/examples/tags создавайте объект без id или с id=0: импорт создаст запись автоматически.
Новые элементы (без id / с id=0) разрешено добавлять только в файле, где meta.language == meta.default_language.
